rights_and_safety.lgbtq_notes:
ILGA/ILGA-Europe rank Portugal among the top-tier countries with comprehensive
  protections for sexual orientation, gender identity, and race.
rights_and_safety.abortion_notes:
National law protects abortion through the first 12 weeks, giving statutory
  support for access in Portugal.
rights_and_safety.hate_crime_notes:
Hate crime laws explicitly cover sexual orientation, gender identity, and
  race, backed by regular reporting on enforcement.

healthcare.access_notes:
Portugal’s SNS plus private providers enroll expats after legal residency
  registration; GP visits average €5–€20 and private premiums run €35–€120 per month.
healthcare.quality_notes:
Cost comparisons keep Portugal ahead of Spain while the SNS maintains high
  quality, keeping monthly budgets between €740 and €1,500 for single residents.

special_regimes.name: NHR 2.0 (TISRI)
special_regimes.notes:
NHR grants a 20% flat rate on qualifying Portuguese-source income, 10% on foreign
  pensions, and a ten-year window for new residents; pair with the U.S. treaty to combine FEIE/FTC
  strategies.

pathways.name: D7 Passive Income Visa

income_requirement.notes: SEF requires at least €920/month of passive income plus proof of savings.

processing_time_months.notes: Processing typically takes six months or longer.

pathways.notes:
Residency leads to PR after five years while the 2025 reform extends citizenship to ten
  years; maintain income and integration evidence.

pathways.name: EU Blue Card / Skilled Worker Visa

income_requirement.notes: Salary must meet the Portuguese EU Blue Card threshold (confirm with SEF).

processing_time_months.notes:
Portugal does not publish a fixed timeline; applicants should consult SEF for current
  cadences.

pathways.notes:
Time spent on the permit counts toward the standard five-year PR and ten-year
  citizenship clocks.

pathways.name: EU Asylum Procedure (SEF)

income_requirement.notes: No income requirement; claims rest on protection grounds.

processing_time_months.notes:
Border procedures target 12-week decisions for safe-country/low-recognition cases while
  regular procedures aim for six months.

pathways.notes:
Applicants must be physically present to lodge with SEF and follow the EU Migration and
  Asylum Pact flow.

pathways.name: Grandparent Citizenship

income_requirement.notes: No income threshold; documentation must prove a Portuguese grandparent.

processing_time_months.notes: Timing depends on consular processing of identity and lineage documents.

pathways.notes: Submit grandparental proof to a consulate or SEF to secure the citizenship pathway.

diff_summary.notes:
Documented the structured safety indicators, captured D7/Blue Card/asylum/ancestry
  pathways, added healthcare/tax summaries, and flagged medium volatility tied to the 2025
  citizenship reform.

issues.message:
Portugal’s 2025 reform extended the citizenship residency requirement to ten years, so
  policy volatility stays medium until the new timeline stabilizes.
